name: security
description: Core security standards for all projects

observations.title: Never hardcode secrets, API keys, or passwords in source code
facts: Use environment variables or secrets managers
Check for .env patterns, API key formats, private key headers
Block: AWS keys (AKIA...), GitHub tokens (ghp_...), generic passwords

observations.title: Use parameterized queries — never concatenate user input into SQL
facts: SQL injection is consistently OWASP #1
Use prepared statements or ORM query builders
Block: string concatenation in SQL queries with user input

observations.title: Sanitize all user input before rendering in HTML
facts: Escape HTML entities: < > & " '
Use framework-provided escaping (React JSX, template engines)
Block: innerHTML with unsanitized user data, dangerouslySetInnerHTML with raw input

observations.title: Never use eval() or Function() constructor with user input
facts: eval() executes arbitrary code
Same applies to new Function(), setTimeout with strings
Block: eval(userInput), Function(userInput)

observations.title: Validate and sanitize file paths to prevent directory traversal
facts: Normalize paths with path.resolve()
Verify resolved path starts with expected base directory
Block: direct use of user input in file operations without validation

observations.title: Use HTTPS for all external requests and API calls
facts: Never use http:// for API endpoints in production code
Verify TLS certificates (don't disable verification)
Warn: http:// URLs (except localhost)

observations.title: Hash passwords with bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 — never MD5/SHA
facts: Use purpose-built password hashing with salt
Minimum cost factor: bcrypt 12, Argon2 recommended
Block: MD5/SHA for password storage, plaintext password comparison

observations.title: Set HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite flags on session cookies
facts: HttpOnly prevents XSS cookie theft
Secure requires HTTPS
SameSite=Lax or Strict prevents CSRF

observations.title: Rate limit authentication endpoints
facts: Prevent brute force and credential stuffing
Use progressive delays or hard lockout after N attempts
Apply rate limiting by IP and by account

observations.title: Never expose stack traces or internal errors to clients
facts: Return generic error messages in production
Log detailed errors server-side only
Block: sending error.stack or raw exception messages in HTTP responses

observations.title: Validate Content-Type and file content for uploads
facts: Check magic bytes, not just file extension
Limit file size
Store uploads outside webroot with generated filenames

observations.title: Use Content-Security-Policy headers
facts: Restrict script sources to prevent XSS
Avoid unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval
Use nonces for necessary inline scripts

observations.title: Implement proper CORS configuration
facts: Never use Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * with credentials
Whitelist specific origins
Validate Origin header on server side

observations.title: Validate JWT tokens: check algorithm, expiry, issuer, audience
facts: Prevent 'none' algorithm attack
Short-lived access tokens (5-15 min)
Validate all claims before trusting payload

observations.title: Prevent Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
facts: Block requests to private IP ranges from user-supplied URLs
Use allowlists for permitted domains
Resolve DNS before connecting to prevent rebinding attacks
